response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior full-stack developer with genuine systems-programming depth (DLang low-latency microservices at millions of requests per second) and a strong testing and mentoring culture, but he does not meet the role's core minimum qualifications. His documented C++ experience is roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — far short of the required 8 years — and C++ is absent from his own skills list. His career centers on web and product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than leading large-scale cloud compute infrastructure, and he shows no formal technical-leadership track owning cross-team platform strategy.
strengths: Real low-latency distributed-systems work at Dunnhumby (async I/O, caching, high-throughput microservices)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer via workshops, courses, and long-running open-source leadership (GISCollective)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement (~7 months professional C++; not listed in his skills)
Limited large-scale cloud compute platform experience; cloud exposure is mostly AWS CDK and Kubernetes deployment of a small platform
No formal technical-lead role managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment at an infrastructure organization's scale
